generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T GYMNOCLADUS. Gymnocladus Canadensis, Linn. Natural order , Leguminosæ. Common names , Chicot, Coffee tree of Kentucky. Preparation , Tincture of the fresh pulp in the pods around the seeds. Authorities. Provings collected by Dr. C. Hering, N. Am. J. of Hom., 1, 1851; 1 , Charles Sellers ate a small portion of the pulp; 2 , same, took two doses each of about 1/6 of a drop; 3 , Dr. Hering, effects of exhalations while preparing tincture; 4 , same effects of handling the moist pulp; 5 , F. Husemann, proving (dose not given); 6 , J. Schmid; 7 , Schmid's son, Max; 8 , Dr. Daniel Jauncy took 3 drops of tinct. first day; 4 drops, second day; 4 drops, twenty-second day; 2 drops, twenty-third day; 6 drops, twenty-fifth day; 9 , Dr. J. R. Coxe, Jr., took tincture daily for ten days, 3 to 25-drop doses; 10 , Dr. J. E. Gross took 5 drops of tincture, fasting in morning; 11 , Dr. J. B. Munsey took repeated doses of tincture for four days, 3 to 30-drop doses. MIND. ► Emotional. ► Indifferent to what happens (after second dose, second day), [11] . ► Intellectual. ► Cannot think or study; forgets what he knew (after first dose, third day), [11] . ► Cannot think quickly, compelled to gaze at an object some time to comprehend it (after first dose, second day), [11] . ► Cannot study (after second dose, second day), [11] . ► Could not take an abstract of the lectures in the afternoon (fourth day), [11] . HEAD. ► Vertigo. ► Vertigo at times (ten hours after first dose, second day), [11] . ► Dizziness (after first dose, second day), [11] . ► Dizziness, with dimness of sight, [10] . ► General Head. ► Head swelled, as in erysipelas, for several days; compelled to wash with Arnica to obtain relief (fifth day), [11] . [10.] ► Head feels heavy and eyes dull (two hours after second dose, second day), [11] . ► Desire to lean the head on something (there hours after third dose, first day), [11] . ► Pain in the head, mostly over the eyes (eleventh day), [9] . ► Pain in the head and stitches in the bowels, in the umbilical region at times (twenty-fifth day), [8] . ► Fulness of the head, [10] (in morning, fourth day), [11] . ► Feeling as if the head would burst, [10] . ► Head feels tight, as if bound up, [10] . ► Headache, at 12 M. (twenty-sixth day), [8] . ► Dull headache for two hours (after first dose); slight headache (after second dose, first day), [11] . ► Slight headache (fourth day); intense headache; never had headache in his life before (from fifth to tenth day), [9] . [20.] ► Bruised feeling of head, mostly left side, in the morning (third day), [11] . ► Forehead. ► Dulness in forehead and left eye (one hour after second dose, third day), [11] . ► Dull pains in the forehead, mostly over the left eye, [10] . ► Headache, fulness, and pressure in and over the eyes, in the forehead, and extending to the top of the head, with occasional shooting pains (after four hours and a half), [2] . ► Headache over the eyes, first on the left, and after awhile also on the right side; headache over the right temple, [4] . ► Feeling in the sinciput as if the eyes were pushed forward (from fifth to tenth day), [9] . ► Sharp, violent pain in the forehead, over both eyes, very near the eye, lasting three minutes or longer; extending triangularly into the upper part of the head, mostly on the right side, [6] . ► Temples. ► Pain in the left temple (after first dose, second day), (morning, fourth day), [11] . ► Pain in left temple and head (fifth morning), [11] . ► Pain in temple to the ear and top of the head, sometimes shoots through to the right side (after three hours, fourth day), [11] . [30.] ► Soreness in left temple (one hour after second dose, third day), [11] . ► Vertex. ► Dull pain on the top and right side of the head, [10] . ► Parietals. ► Pain in the left side of the head (two hours after first dose, third day), [11] . ► Pain in the left side of the head and right breast, and in the arms (two hours after third dose, first day), [11] . EYE. ► Desire to rub the eyes, must rub them (after three hours, fourth day), [11] . ► Sensation of agglutination of left eye, a desire to rub or scratch it externally, in the morning (third day), [11] . ► Heat and burning in the eyes, at 12 M. (twenty-sixth day), [8] . ► Burning in the left eye, as if it would be forced out (after three hours, fourth day), [11] . ► Stinging pains in the right eye, [10] . ► Eyes sore (fifth morning), [11] . ► Brow and Orbit. [40.] ► Dull heavy pain between the eyes, [10] . ► Violent throbbing over the left eye, [10] . ► Lachrymal Apparatus. ► Water from the eyes (after three hours, fourth day), [11] . ► Ball. ► Pain in left eyeball and temple (two hours and a half after second dose, second day), [11] . ► Pressure in the eyeball and chest, in breast and sternum (fifth morning), [11] . NOSE. ► Frequent, violent sneezing, which originated very high up in the nose, [3] . ► A peculiar sneezing, as if very soft, and each exhalation slow (second or third day), [7] . FACE. ► Pain and soreness in the left malar bone, as if from a contusion, in the morning (third day), [11] . MOUTH. ► Teeth. ► Reappeared, a great sensibility of the teeth; more on the left side, and most in the upper teeth; the slightest draught of cool air sets the teeth aching; cold drinking hurts most severely (first day); much worse (second day); disappearing (third day), [4] . ► Pain in a plugged tooth (fifth day), [11] . ► Tongue. [50.] ► Tongue coated bluish white (fifth morning), [11] . ► General Mouth. ► Scraping burning in the roof of the mouth, extending to the uvula (after four hours and a half), [2] . ► Burning-drawing sensation in roof of mouth, extending to the uvula, soon passing away, [1] ; (after half an hour), [2] . ► Soreness in palatine arch (one hour after second dose, third day), [11] . ► Saliva. ► Water in the mouth (two hours after third dose, first day), [11] . ► Water in the mouth, almost to vomiting (after three hours, fourth day), [11] . ► Accumulation of mucus in the mouth, and sticking in the throat (two hours after third dose, first day), [11] . ► Taste. ► Taste of medicine in mouth and throat for two hours (after five minutes, third day), [9] . ► Bad taste in mouth (fifth day), [11] . ► Bitter taste, very soon, [4] . THROAT. ► Objective. [60.] ► Mucus in throat, frequent hawking (second day), [11] . ► Hawking shooting in throat (fifth day), [11] . ► Subjective. ► Burning in throat and stomach for three hours (fourth day), [9] . ► Sticking in throat (ten hours after first dose, second day), [11] . ► Throat smarts and burns (after second dose, third day), [11] . ► Tickling in the throat, producing a cough, which continued to increase all day, and in the evening was one of the worst hard dry coughs I ever had (second day), [2] . ► Drawing pain in the uvula and tonsils, particularly the right side; right tonsil very much inflamed, and of a purple color (after four hours and a half), [2] . ► Burning in sophagus, immediately (third day), [9] . ► Squeezing pain on the left sterno-cleido-mastoideus, high up, of short duration, [5] . STOMACH. ► Appetite. ► Slight appetite for breakfast; bread tastes dry (fifth morning), [11] . [70.] ► Loss of appetite (fourth to ninth day); has not eaten as much in the last five days as at an ordinary breakfast, his principal meal (tenth day), [9] . ► No appetite in the morning (fourth day), [11] . ► Eructation. ► Eructations (fourth day); increasing to ninth day, [9] . ► Eructations, slight burning in the scrobiculus cordis, at 11 P.M., continuing till midnight (second day), [9] . ► Gulping of wind, [11] . ► Gulping of wind and uneasiness of bowels (two hours after third dose, first day), [11] . ► Spitting up of sour water (from fifth to tenth day), [9] . ► Heartburn. ► Heartburn, all day (fourth day), [9] . ► Nausea. ► Nausea (second and third days), [11] . ► Nausea, after eating, lasting three hours, [10] . [80.] ► Nausea, with unpleasant feeling in the stomach (fifth day), [11] . ► Stomach. ► Pain in the stomach (one hour after second dose, third day), [11] . ► Heat in the stomach and sour watery eructations, the last continuing till 11 A.M. (thirteenth day), [9] . ► Hot feeling in the stomach and bowels (two hours after second dose, second day), [11] . ► Slight burning in pit of stomach, all day (third day), [9] . ► Burning in the stomach very intense (from fifth to tenth day); he never had the most trifling uneasiness there before, [9] . ► Constant burning in the stomach, circumscribed, about the size of a dollar (eleventh day), [9] . ► Sensation of fulness in the stomach, with slight nausea (after four hours and a half), [2] . ABDOMEN. ► Hypochondria. ► Pain in the left side, as though the spleen was swollen (fifth day), [11] . ► Umbilical. ► Commotion in umbilical region (fifth day), [11] . [90.] ► Pain in umbilical region (after second dose, first day), [11] . ► Pain in umbilicus, at 11 o'clock, lasting two hours (twenty-third day), [8] . ► General Abdomen. ► Flatulency and rumbling in the bowels (sixth day), [8] . ► Frequent flatus in the abdomen, pressing on the bladder, [3] . ► Feels as if he had taken a purgative, in the morning (fourth day), [11] . ► Feels as if diarrha would come on (two hours after first dose, third day), [11] . ► Feeling in the abdomen as if diarrha would follow; hard stool, the last part somewhat softer (after three hours); and afterwards an aching fulness in the rectum, about five or six inches above the anus, all around; the whole sensation occupying a space of the size of the fist, [4] . ► Pain in the bowels (after three hours, fourth day), [11] . ► Pain and uneasiness in the bowels and stomach (twenty-third day), [8] . ► Pain in the bowels and flatulency (twenty-second day), [8] . [100.] ► Dull pain in the bowels (twenty-sixth day), [8] . ► Pressing pain on the left side of the abdomen, disappearing after eructation, [5] . ► Stitches and pain in the bowels (second day), [8] . ► Soreness of abdomen (two hours after first dose, third day), [11] . ► Tenderness of abdomen (one hour after second dose, third day), [11] . ► Hypogastrium. ► Pain in the lower part of the abdomen (after four hours and a half), [2] . ► Pinching in the lower part of the abdomen, towards the left side, [5] . ► Pain in the right inguinal glands, [5] . RECTUM AND ANUS. ► Disposition to go to stool, with inability to do so, lasting for three or four hours, [10] . STOOL. ► Bowels relaxed, in the morning (fourth day), [11] . [110.] ► Bowels constipated (fourth day), [8] . URINARY ORGANS. ► Pressure in the bladder, as if large quantities of urine had been retained too long, [3] . ► Desire to urinate frequently; urine in small stream (ten hours after first dose, second day), [11] . ► Urine brown, yellow, turbid, in the morning (fourth day), [11] . SEXUAL ORGANS. ► Itching of glans penis (ten hours after first dose, second day), [11] . ► A long-continuing, increasing sexual desire, and erections at night, [4] . RESPIRATORY ORGANS. ► Smarting in the larynx (after second dose, first day), [11] . ► Now and then a slight cough, not violent nor unpleasant, [7] . CHEST. ► In the left chest, in the middle, is a painful pressure, more the second and less the third day, [1] . ► Sharp pain in the right side, at 11 P.M.; continued about an hour (fourth day); returned again in the morning in the same place, [8] . [120.] ► After walking a few squares, sharp shooting pain in the right side, in the intercostal muscles; the same pain in the left side of the face; walked home and was not able to go out any more that afternoon; slept in the chair from 2 to 4 P.M., then felt better, and had a good appetite for supper; can think and study some (fourth day), [11] . ► Stitches in right breast, and in drawing a breath cannot think, comprehend, nor study; forgets everything (after three hours, fourth day), [11] . ► Shooting in right breast (two hours after first dose, third day), [11] . PULSE. ► Pulse small and quick (ten hours after first dose, second day), [11] . BACK. ► Pain in dorsal vertebræ and head, at 8 P.M. (twenty-fifth day), [8] . ► Pain in the small of the back (after four hours and a half), [2] . EXTREMITIES IN GENERAL. ► Feels it in all his limbs, immediately (after second dose, third day), [11] . SUPERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Dull pain in the left elbow-joint, [10] . ► A most violent pain in the left forearm, in the radius, between the elbow and wrist, as if the bone were crushed or broken; often repeated, [4] . ► Pain in left hand (one hour after second dose, third day), [11] . [130.] ► Pain in ring finger of left hand (fifth day), [11] . ► Burning on the point of the left thumb, [4] . ► Dull pains in the fingers, [10] . ► Pulsation in the left index, as if a panaritium were forming (two hours and a half after second dose, second day), [11] . INFERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Knee. ► Pain in left popliteal region (one hour after second dose, third day), [11] . ► Sticking pain in the popliteal region of the left leg, in walking, in the forenoon (third day), [11] . ► Stinging pain in the left knee-joint, [10] . ► Leg. ► Pain in the left leg, from the knee to the ankle (two hours after second dose, second day), [11] . ► Pain in tibia (two hours after first dose, third day), [11] . ► Pain in the left calf, on walking (fifth day), [11] . ► Foot. [140.] ► Dull pains in both feet, [10] . ► The boots are uncomfortable; the feet ache; especially the left; most between the little toe and the external ankle (second day), [4] . GENERALITIES. ► Repugnance to motion (after three hours, fourth day), [11] . ► Desire to lean on something (second day), [11] . ► A peculiar feeling of numbness in the whole body, [10] . ► Feels badly for two hours (after one hour, fourth day), [11] . SKIN. ► Sensation as if flies were crawling over the right side of the face (third day), [8] . ► Itching of the prepuce (two hours after first dose, third day), [11] . SLEEP. ► Drowsiness, at 12 M. (twenty-sixth day), [8] . ► Slept badly (second night), [11] . FEVER. ► Chilliness. [150.] ► Feels as though cold had been taken, wants to be near the fire, cold, chilly (three hours after third dose, first day), [11] . ► Cold, chilly, and pains in the bowels, in the descending colon (two hours after second dose, second day), [11] . ► Heat. ► Forehead hot to the touch (two hours and a half after second dose, second day), [11] . ► Face hot and feels swollen (after three hours, fourth day), [11] . ► Sweat. ► Perspiration of axillary region and palms of hands (two hours after first dose, third day), [11] . indications.text: The seeds and pulp of the pods of Gymnocladus canadensis, Lamarck. Nat. Ord.—Leguminosae. COMMON NAMES: American coffee-bean tree, Coffee tree, Kentucky mahogany.

Botanical Source.—This is a slender and unarmed tree, attaining the height of 50 or 60 feet, with a trunk from 12 to 15 inches in diameter. For about 25 feet from the ground the trunk is straight and simple, and covered with a rough, scaly bark. The leaves are compound, unequally bipinnate, 2 or 3 feet long, and 15 to 20 inches wide; the leaflets 7 to 13 in number, ovate, acuminate, and dull-green, the single leaflets often occupying the place of some of the pinnae. The flowers are large, regular, dioecious, whitish, in axillary racemes, succeeded by pods. Petals 5, oblong, equal, inserted on the summit of the calyx tube. Calyx tubular, 6-cleft, and equal. Stamens 10, short, distinct, inserted with the petals. Style 1. The legume is 8 to 10 inches long, 2 to 2 ½ inches wide, oblong, flattened, curving, pulpy within, and several-seeded. The seeds are from 2 to 4, quite hard, and somewhat egg-shaped, of a dark-olive color externally, slightly compressed, and about ¾ inch in length by ½ inch wide (W.—G.).

History and Chemical Composition.—This tree is indigenous to the United States, and is found growing in rich woods and along rivers and lakes in western New York, Ohio, Indiana, Kentucky, etc. It is known by several names, as Coffee tree, Kentucky coffee tree. The seeds were roasted and used by the earlier settlers instead of coffee. The trunk is naked for some distance above the ground, above which is a rather small but regular head, formed by a few, quite long branches. The wood is quite hard and strong, is reddish or light yellowish, rather fine grained, and susceptible of taking a very fine polish and presenting a most beautiful grained appearance; on this account it is highly prized in architecture and cabinetwork. In our eastern cities it has been represented as a wood from Japan and brought most fabulous prices. The pulp and the seeds of the pods are the parts to be used; the former has some reputation as a fly poison. It is greenish and viscid. The active principle of these is taken up by alcohol, which gives a yellowish-brown tincture, or, if the pulp alone be used, a beautiful green, and, upon standing, crystals are deposited. The tincture has an unpleasant, bitterish taste, followed by a persistent pungent acrimony in the fauces. Rafinesque states that the leaves are purgative and contain a principle, cysticine, of a nauseous, bitter taste. The seeds are said to produce emesis. S. S. Mell (1887) obtained from the seeds a yellowish, saponifiable, fixed oil (specific gravity 0.919) to the extent of 10 per cent. Wax, resin and fat were extracted by ether, some tannin and a glucosid, burning to the taste and possessing a distinctive odor, were abstracted by alcohol. The seeds also contained mucilage, starch, and albuminoids (Amer. Jour. Pharm., 1887, p. 230). J. H. Martin largely confirms these results, but found saponin in all parts of the plant, and concludes that to this principle the physiological activity of the plant is probably due. Tannic and gallic acids are absent in the seeds. The pulp surrounding it contains sugar, tartaric and citric acids, and probably saponin (Amer. Jour. Pharm., 1892, p. 558). The carbohydrates of the fruit were investigated by W. E. Stone and W. H. Fest (Amer. Chem. Jour., 1893, p. 660).

Action, Medical Uses, and Dosage.—The tincture of the pulp and pods, and in some instances of the bark also, has been used with benefit in intermittent fever. More recently it has been tried, and with advantage, in cases of abnormal states of the nervous centers, as indicated, among other symptoms, by impaired sense of touch and vision, numbness, dull headache, apathy, and formication. In one case of locomotor ataxia it proved decidedly beneficial, and is valuable in some of the more serious symptoms resulting from excessive masturbation. Recent reports (Dr. N. G. Vassar) confirm its value as a remedy for spermatorrhoea. Prof. Roberts Bartholow, M. D., investigated physiologically the purified tincture of the leaves as prepared for him by J. U. Lloyd and found it to be very marked in its qualities. It has likewise been recommended in laryngeal cough with chronic irritation of the mucous lining membrane of the air passages, in erysipelas, in all fevers presenting a typhoid condition, in puerperal peritonitis, and in the exanthematous affections. It is certainly deserving the attention of our practitioners. The tincture is best made by taking 2 ounces of the coarsely bruised seed and 1 ounce of the pulp, and adding to them 8 fluid ounces each of water and alcohol; let it macerate 12 or 14 days with frequent agitation, and then filter. One fluid drachm of this is to be added to 3 fluid ounces of water, of which the dose is a teaspoonful, to be repeated every 3 or 4 hours.

Related Species.—Cercis canadensis, Linné. Nat. Ord.—Leguminosae. The Red bud or Judas tree, is a small tree growing in rich woods in the middle states. The flowers expand in early spring before the leaves come out. They are borne in lateral clusters and are of a pale-reddish color. They have an agreeably acid taste, and are often eaten by children. The leaves are simple, acute, cordate, and are supported on slender stalks. The fruit is a dry, brown, flat pod, which hangs on the branches during the winter. The name Judas tree is inapplicable, and the tree is so-called because its relative, the Cercis siliquastrum, abundant in Palestine is said to have been the tree upon which Judas hanged himself. It only required a little further credulity to transfer the notion to the American species. The bark of the root is the preferred part, and is exceedingly astringent, even surpassing oak and hemlock. "When chewed it puckers the mucous membranes of the mouth almost as sensibly as the green fruit of the persimmon tree, or as the seed of the fruit of black haw (Viburnum prunifolium)" (Lloyd, in Drugs and Medicines of North America). Prof. J. U. Lloyd could detect neither alkaloid nor crystalline glucosid, the chief constituent being the tannin (ibid., Vol. II, 124). The leaves and bark of this tree, especially the bark of the root, possess powerfully astringent properties, and maybe administered in cases in which this class of agents is indicated, as in diarrhoea and dysentery, particularly in the chronic forms, and in chronic catarrhal conditions; also recommended as a local application in chronic gonorrhoea, gleet, leucorrhoea, and chronic conjunctivitis, and other affections attended with mucous profluvia.
King's American Dispensatory, 1898, was written by Harvey Wickes Felter, M.D., and John Uri Lloyd, Phr. M., Ph. D.
